meta.title: TRITON - Reverse Engineering the Tricon Controller
meta.description:
Reid Wightman of Dragos looks at the TRITON attack from the perspective of a reverse engineer. 

The big takeaway is once the controller is compromised the code can the physical key switch no longer is an effective control. It could be programmed even if the switch was in the Run position, leaving the asset owner with a false sense of security.

Controllers with compiled logic carry huge risk.

He quickly covers the boot loader, inject.bin and imain.bin. Good stuff in 10 minutes.
